purpose: One execution authority. Outside the task-execution service (and the launcher/persistence/host adapters it owns), no production path may assemble a wake, select a host, author an assignment payload, or resolve a runner id. Ceilings turn one way: lowering a measured count must lower the ceiling in the same change. Raising one requires an explicit justification in the PR.

service.description: The execution authority. These files ARE the service and are exempt by definition.

wake_assembly_outside_service.description: Non-service production files that build a wake intent themselves, or hand store.request_wake to something else as a callable.

legacy_launcher_calls_outside_service.description: Non-service production files that reach the launcher (dispatch.dispatch / start_task / resume_review / dispatch_to_co_fleet / latest_from_task_session) directly instead of through a task-execution command. These bypass the attach/dedupe guard, so each one can still fork a second session.

host_selection_outside_service.description: Non-service production code that picks which host runs a task.

assignment_authoring_outside_service.description: Non-service production code that writes an assignment TOML as a source of truth.

runner_resolution_outside_service.description: Non-service production files that decide which runner is current (resolve_runner_watch / resolve_task_active_runner / latest_dispatch_outcome) instead of reading the SIMPLIFY-1 projection.

known.src/switchboard/storage/repositories/del: mission_status resolves the active runner per linked task — a second resolver beside the projection; owner SIMPLIFY-2

browser_execution_facts.description: static/**.js sites that use execution identity the server owns: assembling a wake payload, or sending a self-chosen runner_session_id to a control/PTY/watch endpoint.

known.static/app.js: Fleet tab wake modal + fleet runner control table; owner SIMPLIFY-11 (the task-modal primary card now reads GET /api/tasks/{id}/execution — UI-58)
known.static/js/runner-session.js: fleet-generic runner control table, the Watch-panel gate read, and the control-delivery poll; owner UI-57 / UI-58 follow-on
known.static/js/proof-console.js: Proof Console picks its own task and runner; owner UI-58 follow-on

task_execution_surface.description: HARD ZERO. The task REST router and the task/task-execution MCP tool modules must never assemble a wake, select a host, author an assignment, or resolve a runner id: every execution fact comes from the service.
